Journal: Knowledge-Based Systems

DOI: 10.1016/j.knosys.2026.116206

Relevance: 0.750 (consider)

Services based on various forms of video streaming deliver content to clients by compressing originally captured videos using video codecs. During this process, due to limitations such as network bandwidth and display device capabilities, the original resolution is often reduced or a higher compression rate is applied, resulting in smaller data sizes being transmitted. Consequently, the video quality experienced by end users is often degraded. To address this issue, numerous AI-based Video Super-Resolution (VSR) techniques have been proposed. However, in addition to their high architectural complexity, many of these models require a large number of input frames or rely on recurrent frameworks, which further complicate both training and inference. These factors present significant challenges for deployment in real-time video services. In this paper, we propose a lightweight VSR model that achieves high performance even on low-quality, highly compressed video content. We propose an adaptive inference method that dynamically selects between lightweight and enhanced processing modules based on the input video’s resolution, thereby improving the suitability of our approach for real-time video streaming applications. Additionally, we visualize the performance of the proposed model with Grad-CAM to demonstrate its effectiveness compared to existing methods.

item.title: QMSANet: A Quaternion Multi-Scale Attention Network for Robust Color Image Denoising
item.dc:creator: Yi Liu
Qi Xie
Yu Guo
Guoqing Chen
Boying Wu
Deyu Meng
Jean-Michel Morel
Qiyu Jin
Michael Kwok-Po Ng
item.description: Existing color image denoising methods often fail to adequately capture correlations among RGB channels, leading to structural blurring and the loss of fine details. To overcome this limitation, we propose QMSANet, a Quaternion Multi-Scale Attention Network designed to explicitly model inter-channel correlations ( i.e. , correlations among RGB channels) throughout the denoising process, thereby enabling stronger noise suppression and more faithful detail reconstruction. Our network is built around three complementary modules: the Quaternion Multi-Scale Sparse Block (QMSB), the Quaternion Stacked Enhancement Block (QSEB), and the Lightweight Quaternion Attention Block (LQAB). These modules form a cohesive processing pipeline. Specifically, the QMSB first extracts sparse multi-scale features, allowing the model to capture contextual information at different granularities. These features are then refined by the QSEB, which enhances deep inter-channel interactions and stabilizes feature propagation to improve representational quality. Finally, the LQAB adapts the refined features through a lightweight, parameter-free attention strategy that selectively highlights the most informative responses with minimal computational overhead. Together, these modules operate sequentially to address key denoising challenges, improving efficiency while reducing incomplete noise removal, detail loss, and edge artifacts. Extensive experiments on standard color image denoising benchmarks show that QMSANet consistently outperforms state-of-the-art models under both synthetic and real-world noise. Moreover, although blind denoisers typically underperform their non-blind counterparts, our blind variant ( i.e. , QMSANet-B) still surpasses most representative methods. Published: 2026-05-16T00:01:21+00:00 Venue: Neural Networks Score: 0.748 (consider)

Authors: Yi Liu; Qi Xie; Yu Guo; Guoqing Chen; Boying Wu; Deyu Meng; Jean-Michel Morel; Qiyu Jin; Michael Kwok-Po Ng

Journal: Neural Networks

Relevance: 0.748 (consider)

Existing color image denoising methods often fail to adequately capture correlations among RGB channels, leading to structural blurring and the loss of fine details. To overcome this limitation, we propose QMSANet, a Quaternion Multi-Scale Attention Network designed to explicitly model inter-channel correlations ( i.e. , correlations among RGB channels) throughout the denoising process, thereby enabling stronger noise suppression and more faithful detail reconstruction. Our network is built around three complementary modules: the Quaternion Multi-Scale Sparse Block (QMSB), the Quaternion Stacked Enhancement Block (QSEB), and the Lightweight Quaternion Attention Block (LQAB). These modules form a cohesive processing pipeline. Specifically, the QMSB first extracts sparse multi-scale features, allowing the model to capture contextual information at different granularities. These features are then refined by the QSEB, which enhances deep inter-channel interactions and stabilizes feature propagation to improve representational quality. Finally, the LQAB adapts the refined features through a lightweight, parameter-free attention strategy that selectively highlights the most informative responses with minimal computational overhead. Together, these modules operate sequentially to address key denoising challenges, improving efficiency while reducing incomplete noise removal, detail loss, and edge artifacts. Extensive experiments on standard color image denoising benchmarks show that QMSANet consistently outperforms state-of-the-art models under both synthetic and real-world noise. Moreover, although blind denoisers typically underperform their non-blind counterparts, our blind variant ( i.e. , QMSANet-B) still surpasses most representative methods.

item.title: Diabetic Retinopathy Lesions Segmentation Using Dual Swin U-Net Transformer with Transpose Decoder and Explainable AI
item.dc:creator: Jegan S
Arjun P
item.description: Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a common eye problem in people with diabetes. If it is not treated early, it get worse and may lead to blindness. To address these problems, this research proposes a Diabetic Retinopathy Lesions Segmentation Using Dual Swin U-Net Transformer with Transpose Decoder and Explainable AI (DRLD-DSUN-SS). Initially, a retinal image is collected from publicly available datasets such as Fine-Grained Annotated Diabetic Retinopathy (FGADR), Indian Diabetic Retinopathy Image Dataset (IDRiD) and Digital Retinal Images for Vessel Extraction (DRIVE), which provides high-resolution fundus images with lesion annotations. Images are pre-processed using Adaptive Fast Desensitized Kalman Filter (AFDKF), which includes resizing, noise removal and normalize pixel intensity value. Data augmentation used for (rotation, flipping, scaling) to improve generalization and normalize values. Outer Totality Cellular Automata (OTCA) automatically assigns pixel-level labels. After pre-processing, the dataset is separated into training, validation and test sets. Dual Swin U-Net Transformer (DS-TransUNet) acts as encoder to extract multi-scale contextual features via hierarchical attention, while the Transpose Decoder (TD) reconstructs high-resolution segmentation maps. Integrated DS-TransUNet-TD model segments diabetic retinopathy lesions such as microaneurysms, hemorrhages, hard and soft exudates and the optic disc. Grad-CAM-based XAI methods generate jet-mapped heatmaps, overlaid on fundus images with lesion masks, enhancing interpretability and aiding clinical diagnosis and decision-making. The proposed DRLD-DSUN-SS model demonstrates a successful segmentation performance of 94.9%, representing an improvement of 4.9% - 7.2% over existing state-of-the-art models DMSF-LSDR, ASHF-OCT-DRD and LAD-PEUNet-RS. The model's Dice coefficient and mean IoU were also shown to improve over these methods by 3.5 - 5.8%. Published: 2026-05-17T14:46:59+00:00 Venue: Knowledge-Based Systems Score: 0.746 (consider)

Authors: Jegan S; Arjun P

Journal: Knowledge-Based Systems

DOI: 10.1016/j.knosys.2026.116231

Relevance: 0.746 (consider)

Diabetic retinopathy (DR) is a common eye problem in people with diabetes. If it is not treated early, it get worse and may lead to blindness. To address these problems, this research proposes a Diabetic Retinopathy Lesions Segmentation Using Dual Swin U-Net Transformer with Transpose Decoder and Explainable AI (DRLD-DSUN-SS). Initially, a retinal image is collected from publicly available datasets such as Fine-Grained Annotated Diabetic Retinopathy (FGADR), Indian Diabetic Retinopathy Image Dataset (IDRiD) and Digital Retinal Images for Vessel Extraction (DRIVE), which provides high-resolution fundus images with lesion annotations. Images are pre-processed using Adaptive Fast Desensitized Kalman Filter (AFDKF), which includes resizing, noise removal and normalize pixel intensity value. Data augmentation used for (rotation, flipping, scaling) to improve generalization and normalize values. Outer Totality Cellular Automata (OTCA) automatically assigns pixel-level labels. After pre-processing, the dataset is separated into training, validation and test sets. Dual Swin U-Net Transformer (DS-TransUNet) acts as encoder to extract multi-scale contextual features via hierarchical attention, while the Transpose Decoder (TD) reconstructs high-resolution segmentation maps. Integrated DS-TransUNet-TD model segments diabetic retinopathy lesions such as microaneurysms, hemorrhages, hard and soft exudates and the optic disc. Grad-CAM-based XAI methods generate jet-mapped heatmaps, overlaid on fundus images with lesion masks, enhancing interpretability and aiding clinical diagnosis and decision-making. The proposed DRLD-DSUN-SS model demonstrates a successful segmentation performance of 94.9%, representing an improvement of 4.9% - 7.2% over existing state-of-the-art models DMSF-LSDR, ASHF-OCT-DRD and LAD-PEUNet-RS. The model's Dice coefficient and mean IoU were also shown to improve over these methods by 3.5 - 5.8%.

item.title: OptiSMR: A unified framework for structured reasoning and self-repairing multi-agent system optimized for generalization
item.dc:creator: Juan Chen
Huibin Wang
Lili Zhang
item.description: Optimization problems are ubiquitous across diverse domains; however, their formulation and solution have long relied heavily on expert knowledge. Although large language models show considerable potential for automating such tasks, their limited ability to handle heterogeneous problems, maintain reasoning robustness, and enable autonomous evolution constrains the realization of genuine optimization generalization. To address these challenges, this study proposes OptiSMR, a unified framework designed to advance optimization generalization. Given natural language problem descriptions, OptiSMR employs a prompt-supervised structured chain-of-thought to decompose problems and extract key components such as parameters, variables, objectives, and constraints. This process constructs a universal structured model capable of representing diverse classes of optimization problems. Building upon this foundation, OptiSMR incorporates a multi-agent collaborative architecture that enhances both accuracy and robustness through task specialization and closed-loop error handling. Furthermore, a graph-localized minimum hitting set repair method based on variable-constraint bipartite graphs reformulates error localization and correction as an interpretable optimization process, enabling precise and efficient automatic repair. Moreover, a semantic-guided self-verification mechanism is integrated to suppress the generation of false feasible solutions and to facilitate continuous experience accumulation and autonomous system evolution. Extensive experiments conducted on 6 real-world datasets covering 16 application domains, including resource scheduling, path planning, and manufacturing, demonstrate that OptiSMR can effectively model linear, nonlinear, and mixed-integer programming problems. The proposed framework achieves an average improvement of 8.3% in solution accuracy over an optimal baseline method. Published: 2026-05-16T15:30:21+00:00 Venue: Knowledge-Based Systems Score: 0.746 (consider)

Optimization problems are ubiquitous across diverse domains; however, their formulation and solution have long relied heavily on expert knowledge. Although large language models show considerable potential for automating such tasks, their limited ability to handle heterogeneous problems, maintain reasoning robustness, and enable autonomous evolution constrains the realization of genuine optimization generalization. To address these challenges, this study proposes OptiSMR, a unified framework designed to advance optimization generalization. Given natural language problem descriptions, OptiSMR employs a prompt-supervised structured chain-of-thought to decompose problems and extract key components such as parameters, variables, objectives, and constraints. Building upon this foundation, OptiSMR incorporates a multi-agent collaborative architecture that enhances both accuracy and robustness through task specialization and closed-loop error handling. Furthermore, a graph-localized minimum hitting set repair method based on variable-constraint bipartite graphs reformulates error localization and correction as an interpretable optimization process, enabling precise and efficient automatic repair. Moreover, a semantic-guided self-verification mechanism is integrated to suppress the generation of false feasible solutions and to facilitate continuous experience accumulation and autonomous system evolution. Extensive experiments conducted on 6 real-world datasets covering 16 application domains, including resource scheduling, path planning, and manufacturing, demonstrate that OptiSMR can effectively model linear, nonlinear, and mixed-integer programming problems. The proposed framework achieves an average improvement of 8.3% in solution accuracy over an optimal baseline method.

item.title: Contrastive flow with controllable augmentation for partial multi-label learning
item.dc:creator: Haixiang Li
Min Fang
Xiao Li
Guizhi Wang
item.description: Partial multi-label learning (PML) aims to train multi-label classifiers from ambiguously labeled data, where the primary challenge lies in disambiguating ground-truth labels from a set of noisy candidates. A key difficulty, often overlooked by existing methods, is the fine-grained discrimination between true labels and highly confusing noise labels that share similar features. Current disambiguation strategies lack explicit contrastive mechanisms for this purpose, while generative augmentation methods focus on synthesizing class-center prototypes without strengthening decision boundaries. To overcome these issues, a novel framework, termed CoCA-Flow (Contrastive and Controllable Augmentation Flow), is proposed. The framework empowers conditional normalizing flows for PML by introducing a likelihood-based contrastive loss for principled label disambiguation. Building upon this, a controllable sample generation strategy is introduced to produce distinct prototype and boundary samples. This enables an asymmetric augmentation scheme for enhanced disambiguation and generation guidance. Experimental results on 15 benchmark datasets demonstrate that CoCA-Flow outperforms seven representative baselines spanning discriminative (CDCR, PLAIN, PML-ED), generative (PML-GAN), and traditional optimization-based (BLS-PML, PML-ND, ML-KNN) paradigms, achieving the best Hamming Loss on 12 out of 15 datasets and the best Average Precision on 10 out of 15 datasets. Published: 2026-05-16T15:21:13+00:00 Venue: Pattern Recognition Score: 0.745 (consider)

Journal: Pattern Recognition

DOI: 10.1016/j.patcog.2026.113959

Relevance: 0.745 (consider)

Partial multi-label learning (PML) aims to train multi-label classifiers from ambiguously labeled data, where the primary challenge lies in disambiguating ground-truth labels from a set of noisy candidates. A key difficulty, often overlooked by existing methods, is the fine-grained discrimination between true labels and highly confusing noise labels that share similar features. Current disambiguation strategies lack explicit contrastive mechanisms for this purpose, while generative augmentation methods focus on synthesizing class-center prototypes without strengthening decision boundaries. To overcome these issues, a novel framework, termed CoCA-Flow (Contrastive and Controllable Augmentation Flow), is proposed. The framework empowers conditional normalizing flows for PML by introducing a likelihood-based contrastive loss for principled label disambiguation. Building upon this, a controllable sample generation strategy is introduced to produce distinct prototype and boundary samples. This enables an asymmetric augmentation scheme for enhanced disambiguation and generation guidance. Experimental results on 15 benchmark datasets demonstrate that CoCA-Flow outperforms seven representative baselines spanning discriminative (CDCR, PLAIN, PML-ED), generative (PML-GAN), and traditional optimization-based (BLS-PML, PML-ND, ML-KNN) paradigms, achieving the best Hamming Loss on 12 out of 15 datasets and the best Average Precision on 10 out of 15 datasets.

item.title: TabFusion: Lightweight early fusion of tabular and image data with graph-convolutional neural networks for skin cancer detection
item.dc:creator: Rashik Iram Chowdhury
Nusrat Kabir Nuha
Muhtasimul Hasan
Mohammad Junayed Hasan
M.R.C. Mahdy
item.description: Multimodal fusion approaches have emerged as critical enablers for accurate cancer detection, particularly in integrating clinical metadata with imaging data. This study introduces the first early fusion architecture that incorporates a Graph Convolutional Network (GCN) for clinical metadata representation before integration with a CNN-based vision backbone for multimodal skin lesion classification, addressing fundamental limitations in existing late and mid-fusion strategies. Our TabFusion framework fuses clinical skin lesion images with patient metadata at the input level, enabling joint representation learning through a lightweight GCN-enhanced multimodal architecture. The proposed ensemble learning strategy combines MobileViT and PVTv2 models, achieving state-of-the-art performance with 91.74% accuracy and 92.30% F1-score on the PAD-UFES-20 dataset, significantly outperforming all existing multimodal approaches. Through knowledge distillation, we transfer this performance to a compact custom student model (TabFusion) that maintains robust classification accuracy of 88.26% and F1-score of 86.62% while reducing computational complexity by over 60%. Explainable AI techniques, including Grad-CAM++ and SHAP, provide in-depth interpretability for both visual and tabular features, enhancing clinical trust and transparency. The proposed framework establishes new benchmarks for multimodal medical image analysis and demonstrates practical viability for deployment in resource-constrained healthcare environments. Codes and implementations are publicly available at: https://github.com/RashikIram/TabFusion-SkinLesion-GCN . Published: 2026-05-16T00:02:22+00:00 Venue: Knowledge-Based Systems Score: 0.744 (consider)

item.title: An end-to-end dual backbone framework for UAV fish disease detection using optimized GateHCO feature fusion mechanism
item.dc:creator: Jiaji Pan
Zehui Liu
Xuanzhi Li
Xianwu Luo
Fengming Huang
Miaolei He
Wei Qin
Yaoyi Cai
Jun Xiao
Yu Li
Hao Feng
item.description: Early detection of fish disease is of great significance to inhibit the transmissible disease. Nowadays, computer vision and deep learning methods serve as the most innovative method to identify fish disease. However, most vision technologies to detect fish disease are limited to 2D objects which focus on the feature extraction from images. The dynamic information in the videos are not fully utilized. In this study, we developed a novel hybrid deep learning method and established a dual-backbone architecture for fish disease detection. The model can both extract static appearance features and dynamic behavioral features simultaneous. In particular, we designed a GateHCO (Gated Heat Conduction) attention module, inspired by the heat conduction process and simulating the information propagation in the data, which achieve an effective cross-modal information interaction at a relatively low computation cost. In addition, we developed a UAV (unmanned aerial vehicle) to collect experimental datasets to validate the prototype method detecting fish with GCHD (grass carp haemorrhage disease). In our collected datasets by the UAV, the proposed hybrid method achieved 93.64 mAP, 55.33 ms, 25.55 G FLOPs. The evaluation indicators are notably superior than general detection models (including YOLOv7, YOLOv10, YOLOv12, etc.), RT-DETR and one-stage spatio-temporal framework. We also confirm its superiority than 3D CNN architectures based on ResNet, ResNeXt, and ShuffleNetV2 as well as other advanced Transformer-based architectures such as the MViT series. Published: 2026-05-16T15:28:40+00:00 Venue: Neural Networks Score: 0.743 (consider)

Nowadays, computer vision and deep learning methods serve as the most innovative method to identify fish disease. However, most vision technologies to detect fish disease are limited to 2D objects which focus on the feature extraction from images. The dynamic information in the videos are not fully utilized. In this study, we developed a novel hybrid deep learning method and established a dual-backbone architecture for fish disease detection. The model can both extract static appearance features and dynamic behavioral features simultaneous. In particular, we designed a GateHCO (Gated Heat Conduction) attention module, inspired by the heat conduction process and simulating the information propagation in the data, which achieve an effective cross-modal information interaction at a relatively low computation cost. In addition, we developed a UAV (unmanned aerial vehicle) to collect experimental datasets to validate the prototype method detecting fish with GCHD (grass carp haemorrhage disease). In our collected datasets by the UAV, the proposed hybrid method achieved 93.64 mAP, 55.33 ms, 25.55 G FLOPs. The evaluation indicators are notably superior than general detection models (including YOLOv7, YOLOv10, YOLOv12, etc.), RT-DETR and one-stage spatio-temporal framework. We also confirm its superiority than 3D CNN architectures based on ResNet, ResNeXt, and ShuffleNetV2 as well as other advanced Transformer-based architectures such as the MViT series.

item.title: Cross-modal Recipe Retrieval via multi-granularity alignment
item.dc:creator: Runqi Zan
Tao Yao
Yuxin Yu
Guorui Sheng
Zongchao Huang
item.description: With the increasing global emphasis on food safety and nutrition management, the cross-modal recipe retrieval technology has become increasingly prominent in the realm of food computing. However, previous methods generally focus on aligning the recipe texts and images with coarse embeddings for learning a sharing space, which overlooks the alignment of main food ingredients between image and text modalities, making them hard to distinguish those foods with similar ingredients and consequent inaccurate searching results. To overcome the challenge, we propose a Cross-modal Multi-Granularity Alignment (CMGA) method for recipe retrieval task. Specifically, a Key Information Enhancement (KIE) module, which incorporates the cross-attention mechanism and Large Language Model (LLM) is first designed to make the text encoder focus on key explicit food ingredient. And then an Image Detail Enhancement (IDE) module is proposed to capture food components and cooking details in images by combining the sparse attention and dynamic feature fusion module. Finally, a bidirectional contrastive loss function, which can make the module focus on hard samples learning is employed to further distinguish similar recipes. Experiments on the Recipe1M dataset show that CMGA outperforms baseline models in the cross-modal retrieval task for recipes. Published: 2026-05-16T22:17:48+00:00 Venue: Neural Networks Score: 0.742 (ignore)

Relevance: 0.742 (ignore)

With the increasing global emphasis on food safety and nutrition management, the cross-modal recipe retrieval technology has become increasingly prominent in the realm of food computing. However, previous methods generally focus on aligning the recipe texts and images with coarse embeddings for learning a sharing space, which overlooks the alignment of main food ingredients between image and text modalities, making them hard to distinguish those foods with similar ingredients and consequent inaccurate searching results. To overcome the challenge, we propose a Cross-modal Multi-Granularity Alignment (CMGA) method for recipe retrieval task. Specifically, a Key Information Enhancement (KIE) module, which incorporates the cross-attention mechanism and Large Language Model (LLM) is first designed to make the text encoder focus on key explicit food ingredient. And then an Image Detail Enhancement (IDE) module is proposed to capture food components and cooking details in images by combining the sparse attention and dynamic feature fusion module. Finally, a bidirectional contrastive loss function, which can make the module focus on hard samples learning is employed to further distinguish similar recipes. Experiments on the Recipe1M dataset show that CMGA outperforms baseline models in the cross-modal retrieval task for recipes.

item.title: Pose-Guided YOLO-CED: A Two-Stage Framework for Robust SMD-PCB Defect Detection in Mobile Manipulators
item.dc:creator: Mingxiao Sun
Qiang Zhao
Qiuyu Zhang
Tiantian Luan
item.description: The stability of printed circuit boards (PCB) and their surface-mount devices (SMD) holds significant importance in industrial production applications. However, when deploying mobile manipulator to replace manual safety inspections in manufacturing environments, challenges persist due to uncertainties in PCB spatial pose, significant variations in SMD dimensions, and computational limitations. Achieving stable identification across all defect categories remains difficult. To address this, this paper proposes a two-stage method for SMD-PCB defect detection in mobile manipulator, combining target pose estimation with YOLO-CED. First, a color attention mechanism is introduced. By associating color features with spatial features, it adaptively assigns appropriate weights to different regions. High-precision planar detection enhances the robustness of point cloud-based pose estimation. Second, an adaptive gating mechanism with learnable parameters is introduced for feature extraction, handling channel weights for three-branch parallel feature acquisition fusion and lightweight spatial weights. Proposing a multi-sensory field feature acquisition mechanism and an edge sharpening feature fusion mechanism based on Laplace operator convolution. Concurrently, a detail restoration branch ensures precise boundary localization by preserving fine features of small objects. Finally, the spatial reshaping branch features are processed through an L2-normalized self-supervised multi-head attention mechanism, and the dual-branch features are fused to produce the final output. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms multiple lightweight models on the self-built HUSTPCB dataset (covering 10 common defect categories), achieving a mean average precision (mAP) of 81.7%. This algorithm was verified on a mobile platform integrated with a robotic arm, demonstrating the application prospects of the mobile manipulator in flexible PCB manufacturing. Meanwhile, its excellent performance on the PKU-Market-PCB dataset proved the universality and transferability of the YOLO-CED. Published: 2026-05-17T14:46:24+00:00 Venue: Neural Networks Score: 0.741 (ignore)

Achieving stable identification across all defect categories remains difficult. To address this, this paper proposes a two-stage method for SMD-PCB defect detection in mobile manipulator, combining target pose estimation with YOLO-CED. First, a color attention mechanism is introduced. By associating color features with spatial features, it adaptively assigns appropriate weights to different regions. High-precision planar detection enhances the robustness of point cloud-based pose estimation. Second, an adaptive gating mechanism with learnable parameters is introduced for feature extraction, handling channel weights for three-branch parallel feature acquisition fusion and lightweight spatial weights. Proposing a multi-sensory field feature acquisition mechanism and an edge sharpening feature fusion mechanism based on Laplace operator convolution. Concurrently, a detail restoration branch ensures precise boundary localization by preserving fine features of small objects. Finally, the spatial reshaping branch features are processed through an L2-normalized self-supervised multi-head attention mechanism, and the dual-branch features are fused to produce the final output. Experimental results demonstrate that the proposed method outperforms multiple lightweight models on the self-built HUSTPCB dataset (covering 10 common defect categories), achieving a mean average precision (mAP) of 81.7%. This algorithm was verified on a mobile platform integrated with a robotic arm, demonstrating the application prospects of the mobile manipulator in flexible PCB manufacturing. Meanwhile, its excellent performance on the PKU-Market-PCB dataset proved the universality and transferability of the YOLO-CED.
